creator: jfc@mit.edu
text:
I applied the patches from main to my (now) 13.3-PRERELEASE system.  The initial error handling worked, converting the filesystem to read-only and failing the system call in progress.

When I unmounted the filesystem my system crashed because the mnt_lockref field was negative.

I can report this crash as a separate bug if it is insufficiently related to the original bug.

From the crash dump analysis:

Unread portion of the kernel message buffer:
MPASSERT mp 0xfffffe03ae86f5c0 failed: mp->mnt_ref > 0 && mp->mnt_lockref >= 0 && mp->mnt_writeopcount >= 0 not true at /usr/home/jfc/freebsd/src/sys/kern/vfs_mount.c:1718 (vfs_op_enter)
panic: invalid count(s): ref 2314 lockref -1 writeopcount 0
cpuid = 17
time = 1705678821

#0 0xffffffff80c1a7d5 at kdb_backtrace+0x65

#2 0xffffffff80bcf813 at panic+0x43

  mnt_vfs_ops = 1
  mnt_kern_flag = 0x4100
  mnt_flag = 0x1001
  mnt_rootvnode = 0
